question: Install Ansible on the control node if it is not already installed.
hint: Use the package manager for Rocky Linux.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
if command -v ansible >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

question: Run an ad-hoc Ansible ping module to verify connectivity to all hosts defined in the default inventory.
hint: Use the command: ansible all -m ping
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
ansible all -m ping 2>&1 | grep -q 'SUCCESS' && exit 0 || exit 1

question: Save the output of the ad-hoc ping command to a file named /tmp/ping_result.txt.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
if [ -f /tmp/ping_result.txt ]; then grep -q 'SUCCESS' /tmp/ping_result.txt && exit 0 || exit 1; else exit 1; fi

question: Run an ad-hoc command to check the SSH server version on the remote host using the ansible command module.
Gather facts from the remote host and verify that the distribution is Rocky Linux.
Use an ad-hoc Ansible command to install the 'tree' package on the Rocky node using the yum module.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
ansible all -m yum -a 'name=tree state=present' > /dev/null 2>&1 && exit 0 || exit 1
